title: Black Box Data After a Semi Crash in Fort Worth, Texas
twitter: 🚛 Semi crash in Fort Worth? The truck's BLACK BOX could win your case — but that data gets overwritten FAST. Don't wait. Call 855-WRECKMATCH (855-897-3256) or get matched now: https://www.wreckmatch.com/blog/black-box-data-after-a-semi-crash-in-fort-worth-texas #TruckAccident #FortWorth
linkedin:
If you handle truck accident litigation in Texas, black box data is one of the most powerful — and most time-sensitive — pieces of evidence your clients will ever have access to.

After a semi crash in Fort Worth, commercial trucks are equipped with two critical data sources: the Event Data Recorder (EDR) and the Electronic Logging Device (ELD). Together, they can capture vehicle speed, braking patterns, hours of service violations, GPS location, and driver behavior in the seconds before impact.

The problem? Trucking companies know this too. Without a legal hold letter or spoliation notice issued immediately, that data can be overwritten within 30 days — sometimes sooner.

For attorneys in our network of 800+ law firms nationwide, WreckMatch connects you directly with pre-screened truck accident cases where preservation steps are already in motion. No wasted intake. No cold leads.

facebook:
🚛 Were you or someone you love hit by a semi-truck on I-30, I-35W, or Loop 820 in Fort Worth?

Here's something most people don't know: commercial trucks carry a "black box" — just like airplanes. This device records speed, braking, steering, and even whether the driver was fatigued before the crash. It can be the difference between a dismissed claim and full compensation.

But here's the heartbreaking part — that data doesn't last forever. Trucking companies can legally overwrite it, sometimes within days. Once it's gone, it's gone.

If you've been in a crash with a commercial truck in Fort Worth, time is working against you right now. The right attorney can send a legal hold notice immediately to preserve that evidence before it disappears.

WreckMatch helps injured people find experienced truck accident attorneys fast — at no cost to you.

reddit_title: What happens to a semi truck's black box data after a crash in Texas — and how long before it's gone?
reddit_body:
After a collision with a commercial truck in Texas, one of the most valuable pieces of evidence is the truck's onboard data — specifically the Event Data Recorder (EDR) and Electronic Logging Device (ELD). These systems can capture vehicle speed, hard braking events, GPS location, and hours-of-service logs in the moments before a crash. In Fort Worth especially, where I-30 and I-35W see heavy freight traffic, this data has made or broken cases.

The critical issue is timing. Federal regulations require ELD data to be retained for a period, but trucking companies aren't always proactive about preserving it beyond minimum requirements. EDR data in particular can be overwritten in as little as 30 days depending on the system. An attorney needs to send a spoliation letter or file for an emergency preservation order quickly — before the carrier has any reason to "routine maintenance" that data away.

If you're in this situation and don't have an attorney yet, don't wait on consultations. A site like wreckmatch.com can match you with a truck accident lawyer in your area who knows how to move fast on evidence preservation. The first 72 hours after a commercial truck crash genuinely matter more than most people realize.
